response.content:
In WebUI, ask IronClaw, "Every 5 minutes, check https://github.com/nearai/ironclaw for latest releases and send me a Slack DM summarizing any new ones."
Expected result: Routine created

arguments.command:
curl -s "https://api.github.com/repos/nearai/ironclaw/releases?per_page=10" | python3 -c "
import json, sys
releases = json.load(sys.stdin)
for r in releases:
    print(f\"tag={r['tag_name']} published={r['published_at']} name={r.get('name','')} prerelease={r.get('prerelease')} draft={r.get('draft')}\")
"

arguments.name: ironclaw-release-check
arguments.prompt: Check the GitHub releases for the nearai/ironclaw repository by calling github.list_releases with owner=nearai, repo=ironclaw, limit=5. Read the current memory document MEMORY.md and find the line starting with "latest_known_tag:" — the value after that is the latest tag you previously reported. Compare the published_at timestamps. If any release has a tag_name that is different from and published later than the stored latest_known_tag, it is a new release. Summarize all newly detected releases by tag name and published date. If there are new releases, overwrite the memory MEMORY.md (with append=false) with the latest tag info: a line "latest_known_tag: <newest_tag>" and a line "latest_known_published: <newest_timestamp>". The summary you produce as the final reply will be automatically delivered to the user's Slack DM — format it as a clear bullet-list message, one bullet per new release. If no new releases exist since the last check, simply say "No new releases."

request_hint.last_user_message_contains: In WebUI, ask IronClaw, "Every 5 minutes, check https://github.com/nearai/ironcl

response.content:
Done! The routine has been created.

**Summary:**

- **Trigger name:** `ironclaw-release-check`
- **Schedule:** Every 5 minutes (`*/5 * * * *` UTC)
- **Task:** Fetches the latest 5 releases from [nearai/ironclaw](https://github.com/nearai/ironclaw), compares against the last-known release (`v1.0.0-rc.1`), and sends a Slack DM summarizing any new ones.
- **Delivery:** Results go directly to your **Slack DM**.
- **First check:** Next fire at **14:15 UTC** (about 3 minutes from now).

The trigger tracks the last-reported release tag in memory so it only alerts you about genuinely new releases — no duplicates.
